ORDER ON DEFENDANTS' PETITION TO STAY THE ACTION AND COMPEL ARBITRATION

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANTS 1883 14TH AVENUE, LLC, RAVI ANAND AND OTIANA INVESTMENTS, LLC'S PETITION TO STAY ACTION AND COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED. THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE IS BROAD ENOUGH TO REQUIRE ARBITRATION OF ALL TORT AND CONTRACT CLAIMS ASSERTED AGAINST THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. (EFUND CAPITAL PARTNERS V. PLESS (2007) 150 CAL.APP.4TH 1311, 1328-1330.) DEFENDANTS RAVI ANAND AND OTIANA INVESTMENTS CAN COMPEL ARBITRATION BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS SUE THEM AS ALTER EGOS OF THE SELLER. (ROWE V. EXLINE (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 1276, 1284.) CCP 1281.2(C) DOES NOT APPLY TO THIS CASE. PARAGRAPH 26B OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT PROVIDES THAT "[E]NFORCEMENT OF THIS AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE SHALL BE GOVERNED BY THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT." "BECAUSE THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT (9 U.S.C. 1 ET SEQ.; FAA) CONTAINS NO PROVISION ANALOGOUS TO SECTION 1281.2, SUBDIVISION (C), THAT SUBDIVISION CANNOT BE APPLIED TO DENY THE ENFORCEMENT OF ARBITRATION CLAUSES GOVERNED BY THE FAA." (GLOSTER V. SONIC AUTOMOTIVE, INC. (2014) 226 CAL.APP.4TH 438, 446.) PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANTS WAIVED THE RIGHT TO ARBITRATE. UNDER THE FEDERAL ARBITRATION ACT, THE LAW FAVORS ARBITRATION AND A WAIVER WILL NOT BE LIGHTLY INFERRED. THE PARTY CLAIMING A WAIVER "BEARS A HEAVY BURDEN OF PROOF." (SAINT AGNES MED. CTR. V. PACIFICARE OF CALIFORNIA (2003) 31 CAL.4TH 1187, 1195.) DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ENGAGED IN DISCOVERY. THE DELAY OF DEFENDANTS IN SEEKING ARBITRATION AND THEIR FILING OF A DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE IS INSUFFICIENT TO CONSTITUTE A WAIVER BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE PREJUDICE. (GROOM V. HEALTH NET (2000) 82 CAL.APP.4TH 1189, 1196-1197.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Substitute True Names For Fictitiously Named Cross-Defendants
Off Calendar Apr-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Guy Wilson, Esq., 707-975-2153, appearing for Cross Complainant William Thomson;
Jennifer L. Supman, Esq., Manning & Kass et al, 415-400-0801, appearing for Defendants Vanguard Properties et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

CROSS COMPLAINANT WILLIAM THOMSON's Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Cross-Complaint To Substitute True Names For Fictitiously Named Cross-Defendants

Defendant and Cross-Complainant William J. Thomson dba Thomson Inspection Service's motion for leave to amend cross-complaint to substitute true names for fictitiously named cross-defendants is granted. Section 474 is to be liberally construed, and "even though the plaintiff knows of the existence of the defendant sued by a fictitious name, and even though the plaintiff knows the defendant's actual identity (that is, his name), the plaintiff is 'ignorant' within the meaning of the statute if he lacks knowledge of that person's connection with the case or with his injuries." (See General Motors Corp. v. Superior Court (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 580, 593-94.) "Although it is true . . . that a plaintiff need not be aware of each and every detail concerning a person's involvement before the plaintiff loses his ignorance [citations], it is equally true that the plaintiff does not relinquish her rights under section 474 simply because she has a suspicion of wrongdoing arising from one or more facts she does know." (Id. at 594-95.) "The plaintiff's knowledge is tested at the time suit is filed, and there is no requirement that the plaintiff exercise reasonable diligence to discover the identity of the defendant after filing the complaint." (Sobeck & Associates v. B & R Investments No. 24 (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 861, 867.) Here, on May 23, 2016, when Thomson filed his cross-complaint, Thomson has shown that he was not aware of facts giving rise to causes of action for indemnity and contribution against Defendants Vanguard Properties, Inc., Rusty Wadatz and Colleen Cotter (the Vanguard Defendants). Moreover, the Vanguard Defendants have not shown any prejudice from Thomson's asserted delay in seeking to amend the cross-complaint, nor could they, as the case was stayed from October 4, 2016 until at least December 7, 2018, and is not set for trial until May 4, 2020. (Cf. A.N. v. County of Los Angeles (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1058, 1066-69 [trial judge reasonably concluded that Doe amendments filed on eve of trial, more than two years after complaint was filed, were prejudicial].) The remaining Defendants (1883 14th Avenue, LLC, Otiana Investments, LLC, Ravi Anand, Spilsted Design Associates, R&D Construction Co., Inc., David Cao, and Anand & Associates) have not filed opposition to the motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Petition To Stay Action And Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Edward L. Seidel of Cooper White & Cooper LLP (415.765.6259) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Jonathan C. Harriman of Coddington Hicks & Danforth (650.592.5400) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, 1883 14th Avenue LLC

Attorney, Andrew Foster Shi of Manning & Kass (415.217.6990) appears as counsel for defendants, Vanguard Properties, Rusty Wadatz and Colleen Cotter

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS 1883 14TH AVENUE, LLC, RAVI ANAND AND OTIANA INVESTMENTS, LLC'S Petition To Stay Action And Compel Arbitration is GRANTED. The arbitration clause is broad enough to require arbitration of all tort and contract claims asserted against the moving defendants. (EFund Capital Partners v. Pless (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 1311, 1328-1330.) Defendants Ravi Anand and Otiana Investments can compel arbitration because plaintiffs sue them as alter egos of the seller. (Rowe v. Exline (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 1276, 1284.) CCP 1281.2(c) does not apply to this case. Paragraph 26B of the parties' agreement provides that "[e]nforcement of this agreement to arbitrate shall be governed by the Federal Arbitration Act." "Because the Federal Arbitration Act (9 U.S.C. 1 et seq.; FAA) contains no provision analogous to section 1281.2, subdivision (c), that subdivision cannot be applied to deny the enforcement of arbitration clauses governed by the FAA." (Gloster v. Sonic Automotive, Inc. (2014) 226 Cal.App.4th 438, 446.) Plaintiffs fail to show that defendants waived the right to arbitrate. Under the Federal Arbitration Act, the law favors arbitration and a waiver will not be lightly inferred. The party claiming a waiver "bears a heavy burden of proof." (Saint Agnes Med. Ctr. v. PacifiCare of California (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1195.) Defendants have not engaged in discovery. The delay of defendants in seeking arbitration and their filing of a demurrer and motion to strike is insufficient to constitute a waiver because plaintiffs fail to demonstrate prejudice. (Groom v. Health Net (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 1189, 1196-1197.) Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Andrew Foster Shi of Manning & Kass et al LLP (415-217-6990) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Vanguard Properties

No other appearance is made in this matter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS VANGUARD PROPERTIES, RUSTY WADATZ AND COLLEEN COTTER'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT as to the causes of action for breach of contract and breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. There is no contractual relationship between plaintiff and moving defendants. The purchase contract, which is attached to the second amended complaint, is not ambiguous nor is it reasonably susceptible to plaintiff's proposed interpretation. The contract clearly provides at paragraph 2C that "[b]rokers are not parties to the Agreement between Buyer and Seller." The provisions of the contract that plaintiffs cite purportedly to create an ambiguity do not create any ambiguity. Coughlin v. Blair (1953) 41 Cal.2d 587, is factually distinguishable. There, the express language of the contract indicated that the parties intended that the agent should be personally liable for surveying and for installing improvements. Here, the express language of the contract indicates that there was no contractual relationship between the brokers and the buyer. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)

MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. EDWARD L. SEIDEL OF THE COOPER WHITE COOPER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-765-6259 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JONATHAN C. HARRIMAN OF CODDINGTON HICKS & DANFORTH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-650-592-5400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS 1883 14TH AVE RUSTY WATZ AND COLLEEN COTTER;
ATTY. ANDREW FOSTER SHI OF MANNING & KASS, TEL. NO. 1-415-217-6990 APPEARING FOR THE VANGUARD DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS 1883 14TH AVENUE, LLC, RAVI ANAND, ANAND & ASSOCIATES, OTIANA INVESTMENTS, LLC AND SPILSTED DESIGN ASSOCIATES DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT- The demurrer to certain causes of action in the first amended complaint (FAC) filed by defendants 1883 14th Avenue, Ravi Anand, Ottiana Investments, LLC, Anand & Associates and Spilstead Design Associates is: a) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, the fourth cause of action for construction defect/negligence/property damage, and the seventh cause of action for breach of warranty and b) overruled as to the fifth cause of action for negligent misrepresentation and concealment, the sixth cause of action for fraud. The FAC fails to adequately allege: 1) the term(s) of the written contract that were allegedly breached; 2) the act(s) and/or omission(s) attributable to each of the demurring defendants that supports liability of each of them under the fourth cause of action; and 5) facts supporting the existence of an implied warranty by a seller of a single family home (compare CACI 1231 and 1232 (implied warranties of sellers of products)). The agency, conspiracy, ratification and alter ego allegations in the FAC are sufficient to withstand a pleading challenge. The allegations that the Vanguard defendants stated that the property was "masterfully renovated," "masterfully designed," and "remodeled with premium materials and techniques" are nonactionable puffery and opinions. However, the allegation that the Vanguard defendants stated that the property was "totally remodeled back to the studs" is an actionable statement. Order signed in open court.
